message:
feat(fleet): integrate pooled FDR selection into fleet session kill recommendations

Previously, fleet session aggregation accepted per-host kill recommendations
at face value. This change pools all kill candidates across hosts and applies
the e-value-based Benjamini-Yekutieli FDR procedure from the decision module
to control the fleet-wide false discovery rate, then downgrades rejected kills
to "review" actions.

Key changes:

- Add PooledFdrStatus struct to SafetyBudget capturing method, alpha level,
  total/selected/rejected kill counts, selection threshold, BY correction
  factor, and per-host selected/rejected breakdowns
- Add optional e_value field to CandidateInfo; when absent, a score-to-evalue
  conversion (clamped odds^3 proxy) provides a monotonic fallback
- New compute_pooled_fdr() collects all kill-action candidates, runs select_fdr
  with EBy method, and returns a HashSet of approved candidate keys
- New effective_action() helper downgrades "kill" → "review" for candidates
  not in the approved set, used consistently in compute_host_summary,
  compute_aggregate, and find_recurring_patterns
- Thread selected_kill_keys through the full aggregation pipeline so action
  counts, recurring patterns, and safety budget all reflect FDR-adjusted
  recommendations
- candidate_key() uses "host_id:pid" format for cross-host uniqueness

This ensures that even when individual host sessions recommend aggressive
kills, the fleet view applies a principled statistical gate before surfacing
them as actionable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
